front: What is Dynamic Method Dispatch in Java?
back: A mechanism in Object-Oriented Programming where a call to an overridden method is resolved at runtime, not compile time. It is how Java achieves runtime polymorphism.

front: What keyword is essential for Dynamic Method Dispatch to work?
back: The 'extends' keyword (or implementation of an interface). It requires an inheritance hierarchy where a superclass reference variable can hold a subclass object.

front: How does the JVM determine which version of an overridden method to execute?
back: The type of the object being referred to (the actual object type at runtime) determines which method is called, not the type of the reference variable.

front: What is the main benefit of using Dynamic Method Dispatch?
back: It allows for writing more flexible and reusable code. A single method call in the superclass can execute different subclass implementations, enabling clean abstraction and easier code maintenance.

front: Can Dynamic Method Dispatch be applied to data members (instance variables)?
back: No. Dynamic Method Dispatch only applies to instance methods. Variable access is always determined by the reference type, not the actual object type.
